steps.title: Use a colon for identification
steps.content: The clause before the blank is complete and refers to a powerful mercantile alliance. The phrase after the blank identifies that alliance as the Hanseatic League, so a colon can introduce the identifying noun phrase.

steps.title: Reject fused and fragment forms
steps.content: Choice B fuses the complete clause with the identifying phrase. Choice C makes the Hanseatic League phrase a sentence fragment, and Choice D uses a semicolon before a noun phrase instead of an independent clause. The colon fits the identification. The answer is A.
